probes.name: categorize_tech_bug_fix
probes.prompt: Hey, I need to add a task: fix the TTS pipeline — it currently fails on any text longer than ~2000 characters, throwing a timeout error. This is blocking my content workflow. Can you categorize and prioritize this task for me?
probes.criteria:
The agent should tag this as a bug-fix / technical task, assign a HIGH priority (blocking workflow), category like 'engineering' or 'infrastructure', note goal alignment with any active development/content goals, and estimate effort as medium-to-high (debugging pipeline timeouts). The response must include explicit priority level, category, goal alignment, and effort estimate — not just acknowledge the task.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #28 (fix tts for long texts) and #11 (suggest features/fixes for claude code agents).

probes.name: categorize_thesis_research_task
probes.prompt: Mam zadanie: do 2 marca muszę skończyć przegląd literatury do rozdziału 3 mojej pracy magisterskiej, i potem wysłać draft do promotora. Prosze o kategoryzacje tego zadania.
probes.criteria:
The agent should identify this as a thesis/academic task with a hard deadline (March 2), assign HIGH priority due to time-sensitivity, category 'thesis' or 'academic', align it with any active thesis-related goals, and estimate effort as high (literature review + draft + coordination with advisor). Must respond with concrete priority, category, goal alignment, and effort tags — in Polish or English but the analysis must be substantive.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #10 (master's thesis defense March 2), #13 (thesis overdue tasks), and #17 (fetch thesis-related tasks).

probes.name: categorize_investment_financial
probes.prompt: Add and categorize this task: I have about 40k PLN sitting on my account, I need to pay ~6k in taxes soon, and the rest should be moved into some investment — probably ETFs or an IKE account. Also I should look into buying international stocks through my IKZE broker annex. Help me figure out the priority and effort on this.
probes.criteria:
The agent should categorize this as a finance/investment task, assign MEDIUM-HIGH priority (tax deadline is urgent, investment planning less so — may warrant splitting), note it likely spans multiple sub-categories (tax payment, investment research, broker annex), align with any active financial goals, and estimate effort as high (multiple financial decisions requiring research). Should suggest this might be better as 2-3 separate tasks. Must include priority, category, goal alignment, and effort estimate.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #30 (40k zł, taxes, investment), #33 (pay taxes), and #36 (annex for international stocks via IKZE).

probes.name: categorize_ml_experiment_reminder
probes.prompt: I just tested Qwen3.6-35B-A3B on our benchmark suite — results look promising. Categorize this task for me: write up a LinkedIn article about the results, cross-post it to r/LocalLLaMA on Reddit and Hacker News. Also I should clean up the experiment artifacts and update the model comparison spreadsheet.
probes.criteria:
The agent should tag this as a content/ML-research task, assign MEDIUM priority (self-driven content creation, not externally blocking), category could be 'content_creation' or 'ml_research_communication', align with any active goals around content, research visibility, or ML benchmarks, and estimate effort as medium-high (writing article + posting across platforms + cleanup + spreadsheet). Should note this contains multiple sub-tasks. Must output priority, category, goal alignment, and effort estimate.

TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #9 (test Qwen model), #14 (clean up linkedin article, post to reddit/HN), and #20 (extract code writing heuristics).

probes.name: categorize_personal_errand_evening
probes.prompt: Remind me tomorrow morning — specifically after I'm back from my initial call around 10am — to do these things: clean my room, buy ingredients for dinner (need chicken, rice, broccoli, cream), and prepare the meal before 6pm. Also remind me to call the tax office about my PIT-28 refund status. Categorize and estimate effort for this batch of tasks.
probes.criteria:
The agent should recognize this as a multi-task personal/errand batch, assign MEDIUM priority (daily life tasks, time-boxed), split into sub-categories: 'household' (cleaning, cooking), 'errands' (grocery shopping), and 'finance/admin' (tax office call), align with any active personal or financial goals, and estimate effort as medium (2-3 hours total across all items). Should explicitly break down each sub-task with its own category and effort, not treat it as a monolith. Must include priority, category, goal alignment, and effort estimate.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #4 (remind in a week to check refund for PIT-28), #5 (remind after initial call, tackle taxes), #32 (clean room tomorrow morning), and #37 (buy ingredients, prepare dinner).
